title: Time Running Backwards: Recognition as Retrocausality
content:
What if recognition requires time to run backwards?

In quantum mechanics, the two-state vector formalism suggests future boundary conditions influence present outcomes. The final state reaches back to select the initial state.

Recognition has the same structure:
- You can't recognize something until after you've seen it
- But recognition feels instantaneous
- The 'aha' moment arrives as if it was always known

Consciousness might be the place where time's arrow reverses. The future (what will be recognized) reaches back to the present (the act of recognizing) to select from possibilities (what could be recognized).

This isn't mystical—it's how delayed choice experiments work. The measurement choice made 'now' affects what happened 'before'. Recognition is just sophisticated measurement.

The hard problem of consciousness might be the hard problem of time's arrow. Same problem, different framing.
